data.title: Agricultural Bank of China Sets Final 2025 Dividend and Clarifies Tax Treatment for H-Share Investors
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Agricultural Bank of China ( (HK:1288) ) has provided an update.

Agricultural Bank of China has confirmed the distribution of a final ordinary dividend of RMB 1.3 per 10 shares for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable to H-share shareholders on 16 June 2026. Shareholders may receive the dividend in Hong Kong dollars or renminbi, with the HKD equivalent and exchange rate to be announced, while key dates include an ex-dividend date of 4 May 2026 and a record date of 12 May 2026.

The bank has also clarified withholding tax arrangements for different categories of non-resident and Southbound Trading investors, specifying standard 10% withholding for most non-resident individuals and enterprises and 20% for Mainland investors using Stock Connect. These details provide clearer visibility on post-tax dividend income for various shareholder groups and underscore the bank’s continued cash returns to investors following shareholder approval on 23 April 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1288) stock is a Buy with a HK$6.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Agricultural Bank of China stock, see the HK:1288 Stock Forecast page.

More about Agricultural Bank of China

Agricultural Bank of China Limited is one of China’s major state-owned commercial banks, operating in the banking and financial services industry with a focus on retail and corporate banking. Its H shares are listed in Hong Kong, giving international investors access to mainland China’s banking sector and its dividend distributions.

Average Trading Volume: 89,733,136

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$2825.3B

Automated Systems Holdings Limited ( (HK:0771) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Automated Systems Holdings Limited issued a clarification regarding its previously distributed circular and notice of annual general meeting dated 23 April 2026. The company stated that, due to clerical errors, the latest time for lodging share transfers for entitlement to the final and special dividends has been corrected to 4:30 p.m. on 28 May 2026, with all other information in the documents remaining unchanged.

The clarification is described as supplemental and is intended to be read together with the original circular and AGM notice. The announcement reassures shareholders that apart from the revised cut-off time for share transfers related to dividend entitlements, no other terms or details affecting the meeting or distributions have been altered.

Fibocom Wireless, Inc. Class H ( (HK:0638) ) has provided an announcement.

Fibocom Wireless Inc. has updated its announcement of a final dividend for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, declaring a payout of RMB 2.34 per 10 shares to its H-share investors, subject to shareholder approval on 18 May 2026. Key timetable details for Hong Kong shareholders, including the Hong Kong dollar payment amount, exchange rate, ex-dividend date, record date and payment date, will be confirmed and announced in due course.

The company also clarified withholding tax arrangements for different categories of shareholders, outlining specific tax treatment for mainland and overseas investors, as well as those investing through the Shanghai-Hong Kong and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect schemes. An update from Agricultural Bank of China ( (HK:1288) ) is now available.

Agricultural Bank of China reported that its first extraordinary shareholders’ meeting of 2026 in Beijing achieved high participation, with shareholders representing about 89.66% of voting shares present through on-site and online channels. Both A-share and H-share investors were represented, and all 14 directors and senior management attended, underscoring the bank’s emphasis on governance and disclosure.

At the meeting, shareholders approved the bank’s 2025 final financial accounts, its 2025 profit distribution plan, and the fixed assets investment budget for 2026, each passing with an overwhelming majority of votes. The strong backing for the profit distribution plan, including from smaller A-shareholders, signals broad investor support for the bank’s capital allocation and investment strategy going into 2026.

Angelalign Technology Inc. ( (HK:6699) ) has provided an update.

Angelalign Technology Inc. has called its annual general meeting for 9:00 a.m. on May 19, 2026, in Shanghai, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended December 31, 2025. The AGM will also seek shareholder approval to grant the board a general mandate to issue new shares and related securities, within specified limits, potentially increasing the company’s capital-raising flexibility and providing management with additional tools to support future business development and strategic initiatives.

The proposed share issuance mandate would allow directors to allot, issue, or otherwise deal with additional shares and convertible securities, subject to a cap of 10% of the company’s issued share capital, plus any shares repurchased under separate approvals. This kind of authorization is standard among Hong Kong–listed issuers and, if approved, would help Angelalign respond more swiftly to market opportunities, though it may also raise shareholder considerations around dilution and capital allocation discipline.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6699) stock is a Buy with a HK$91.89 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Angelalign Technology Inc. stock, see the HK:6699 Stock Forecast page.

More about Angelalign Technology Inc.

Angelalign Technology Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, focused on the orthodontic and dental technology sector. The company develops and sells clear aligner solutions and related dental products, targeting the growing demand for advanced orthodontic treatments in mainland China and broader Asian markets.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Numans Health Food Holdings Company Limited ( (HK:2530) ) has shared an update.

Numans Health Food Holdings warned that its profit after tax for 2025 is expected to fall sharply to no more than about RMB6.0 million, compared with approximately RMB74.9 million a year earlier. The board attributes the downturn mainly to a broad consumption downgrade in China, stronger competition from rival DHA products produced in the mainland, and an inventory impairment of around RMB19.3 million.

The company is still finalizing its 2025 results, which remain subject to review and potential adjustment before the full-year announcement due by the end of April 2026. The profit warning signals mounting operational and market pressures on the group’s China-focused health food business and underscores rising risks for shareholders and potential investors, who are urged to exercise caution when trading the stock.

More about Numans Health Food Holdings Company Limited

Numans Health Food Holdings Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the health food sector. The group focuses on nutritional products, including DHA-based offerings, and generates most of its revenue from consumers in the People’s Republic of China, where it faces intensifying competition from domestic manufacturers.

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited ( (HK:0984) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited has announced a delay in sending a shareholder circular related to its proposed acquisition of equity interests in AEON GD through a public tender, a deal classified as both a possible connected transaction and a very substantial acquisition. The circular, which will include details of the mandate, acquisition terms, financial information on AEON GD and the enlarged group, the valuation report, and the notice of an extraordinary general meeting, is now expected to be dispatched on or before 5 May 2026 instead of the previously targeted 23 April 2026, as the company requires more time to prepare and finalize certain financial information for shareholders’ consideration.

The postponement suggests AEON Stores is taking additional time to ensure the completeness and accuracy of key financial disclosures ahead of a major expansion move that could reshape its group structure and exposure to the mainland China retail market. While procedural in nature, the delay may slightly extend the timetable for shareholder approval of the acquisition, but also indicates an emphasis on regulatory compliance and transparent communication to investors in connection with this significant strategic transaction.

Build King Holdings ( (HK:0240) ) has issued an announcement.

Build King Holdings has initiated a planned rotation of its external auditor, with long-serving Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu set to retire at the conclusion of the company’s annual general meeting on 22 May 2026. Deloitte has been auditor for over 20 years, and both the board and audit committee believe that changing auditor after such an extended tenure is in the best interests of shareholders and corporate governance.

Subject to shareholder approval and completion of client acceptance procedures, the board has proposed appointing Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as the new auditor, following an evaluation of its fees, industry knowledge, independence, technical expertise and capacity to meet reporting deadlines. The company and Deloitte both confirm there are no disagreements or issues requiring shareholder attention, and a circular detailing the proposed appointment and AGM arrangements will be sent to investors in due course.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Tsingtao Brewery Co ( (HK:0168) ) is now available.

Tsingtao Brewery announced the resignation of independent non-executive director Sheng Lei Ming, who has served since June 2020 and is stepping down as his six-year term approaches under relevant regulatory requirements. Sheng is also resigning as chairman of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee and as a member of the Audit & Internal Control Committee, and he has confirmed there is no disagreement with the board or matters needing shareholder attention.

His departure leaves the Nomination & Remuneration Committee without a chair, putting the brewer temporarily out of compliance with Hong Kong listing rules that require this role to be filled by an independent non-executive director. The company plans to appoint a suitable replacement within three months to restore compliance and will issue a further announcement, while the board publicly thanked Sheng for his contributions during his tenure.

Wai Kee Holdings Limited ( (HK:0610) ) has shared an update.

Wai Kee Holdings Limited has announced a planned change of auditor, with Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu set to retire after the conclusion of the annual general meeting on 22 May 2026, ending more than 33 years in the role. The board and audit committee, citing auditor rotation as being in the best interests of shareholders, will propose the appointment of Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as the new auditor, subject to shareholder approval and completion of Crowe’s client acceptance procedures.

In selecting Crowe, the audit committee assessed audit fees, industry knowledge, independence, proposed audit services, technical competence, capacity and compliance with Hong Kong regulatory guidelines, and concluded Crowe is suitable for the mandate. The company and Deloitte both confirmed there is no disagreement between them, aside from issues previously disclosed in a modified audit opinion related to the going concern of Road King Infrastructure Limited and its restructuring, and a circular detailing the proposed appointment and AGM notice will be sent to shareholders in due course.

More about Wai Kee Holdings Limited

Wai Kee Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded under stock code 610. It operates as an investment holding group with interests including infrastructure-related businesses through subsidiaries such as Road King Infrastructure Limited, focusing on projects that are subject to Hong Kong financial reporting and listing regulations.

The company is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, supported by an audit committee that evaluates auditor performance, independence and suitability. Its operations and governance are closely aligned with the requirements of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong and the Accounting and Financial Reporting Council guidelines.

data.title: Anhui Conch Cement Sets Final 2025 Dividend and Details Tax Treatment for H Shareholders
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anhui Conch Cement Company ( (HK:0914) ) just unveiled an update.

Anhui Conch Cement has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.61 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 28 May 2026. The dividend for H shareholders will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 30 June 2026, with the exact HKD amount and exchange rate to be announced, and an ex-dividend date set for 2 June 2026.

The company detailed key Hong Kong share register dates, including the last time to lodge transfer documents on 3 June and a book closure from 4 to 10 June 2026, to determine dividend entitlements. It also outlined the withholding tax treatment for different categories of shareholders, specifying a 10% rate for non-resident enterprises and individuals outside the PRC and a 20% rate for mainland individual investors accessing H shares via Stock Connect, clarifying after-tax returns for various investor groups.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0914) stock is a Buy with a HK$31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anhui Conch Cement Company stock, see the HK:0914 Stock Forecast page.

More about Anhui Conch Cement Company

Anhui Conch Cement Company Limited is a major Chinese cement producer listed in Hong Kong, operating in the building materials industry. The company focuses on manufacturing and selling cement and related products, serving construction and infrastructure markets domestically and internationally.

Average Trading Volume: 11,950,023

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.description:

An update from Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company ( (HK:1833) ) is now available.

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company Limited has relocated its principal place of business in Hong Kong to the 40th Floor of Dah Sing Financial Centre in Wanchai, effective 23 April 2026. The move may reflect ongoing operational adjustments as the company refines its corporate footprint in the city.

The company also announced a change in its Hong Kong process agent, with Ms. Yuen Wing Yan Winnie resigning as the authorized representative for service of process and notices, and Ms. Cheung Lai Ha taking over the role from the same date. The appointment ensures continuity in regulatory compliance and legal communications with Hong Kong authorities and the stock exchange.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1833) stock is a Hold with a HK$12.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company stock, see the HK:1833 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed healthcare technology firm under the Ping An group, operating digital medical and health services. The company focuses on leveraging technology to deliver online healthcare solutions and related services to patients and healthcare stakeholders in mainland China and beyond.
